text.title: pytest Parametrize — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-pytest-parametrize application must obey.

rule.statement: When ≥3 tests share the same body but differ only in input/expected, fold them into one @pytest.mark.parametrize.
rule.rationale: Single body = single source of truth; adding a case is one tuple, not a new function.

rule.statement: Provide explicit ids for parametrize cases when failure messages matter. Use the ids= argument or pytest.param(id='...').
rule.rationale: Generated ids like 'test_x[0-1-True]' are unreadable; explicit ids make CI failures debuggable.

rule.statement: Use pytest.param(*values, marks=pytest.mark.xfail, id='...') for per-case marks (skip, xfail).
rule.rationale: Per-case marks let you flag known-bad inputs inline without splitting the parametrize into two.

rule.statement: Stack @pytest.mark.parametrize decorators to express cartesian products of dimensions.
rule.rationale: Stacked params multiply: 3 × 4 = 12 cases; cleaner than authoring 12 tuples by hand.

rule.statement: Use indirect=['param'] to feed values through a fixture (e.g., factory) instead of inline.
rule.rationale: Indirect lets fixtures compute the actual test object from the parameter; useful for DB-backed objects.
